Following that, AP is progressively added while continuously mixed in a water bath at 80 °C. Finally, the resulting viscous mixture is poured into a glass petri dish and then cured for 6 h at 80 °C.

material_input.summary: Modified Hummers-Offeman method: 20 g graphite + 10 g NaNO3 + 550 mL H2SO4, heated to 318 K, cooled to 283 K, 66 g KMnO4 added below 293 K, temperature ≤313 K, stirred 8 h, 1 L H2O added below 323 K, 70 mL 30% H2O2, diluted to 40 L, washed.
material_input.evidence_sentence: GO was prepared by a modified Hammers-Offe-man method [6, 21]. Twenty grams of natural graphite GK-1 and 10 g of NaNO3 were placed in a 2 L three-neck flask equipped with a mechanical stirrer and a thermometer; 550 mL of concentrated H2SO4 was poured into the flask. The resulting mixture was heated in a water bath to 318 K under continuous stirring until the blue color of graphite bisulfate occurred. The resulting suspension was cooled in an ice bath to 283 K; 66 g of KMnO4 was slowly added to it, while maintaining the temperature of the reaction mixture below 293 K. After the addition of KMnO4, the ice bath was removed; the temperature of the mixture was allowed to rise no higher than 313 K. After thickening of the mixture, the stirring was stopped for ~8 h. The mixture was cooled in an ice bath to 283 K; 1 L of bidistilled water was slowly added to the mixture, while maintaining the temperature of the mixture below 323 K. The thoroughly stirred suspension was transferred into a 3 L beaker; 70 mL of 30% H2O2 was added dropwise under stirring. The resulting bright yellow suspension of GO was diluted with bidistilled water to 40 L and washed by decantation until the negative reaction of the mother liquor for SO42– ions (with respect to BaCl2).

classification.evidence: GO was prepared by a modified Hammers-Offe-man method [6, 21]. Twenty grams of natural graphite GK-1 and 10 g of NaNO3 were placed in a 2 L three-neck flask...

material_input.evidence_sentence: FGO was prepared from GO as follows: 0.5 g of dried GO was dispersed in 200 mL of butanol-1 in an ultrasonic bath for 3 h to obtain a homogeneous mass of the exfoliated GO; 50 mL of the respective amine was added to the mass; the resulting mixture was refluxed for 3 h. material_input.summary: 300 mg sample 2 suspended in 40 mL ethanol, sonicated at 318 K (22 kHz) for 15 min. PdCl2 solution (25 mg in 10 mL ethanol + 0.6 mmol HCl) added dropwise. Stirred 45 min. Purged with H2, reduced with NaBH4 (100 mg) at 318 K for 30 min. Filtered, washed until Cl- negative, dried at 318 K for 2 h.
material_input.evidence_sentence: To prepare the Kt-2 catalyst, a 300 mg weighed portion of sample 2 (Table 1) was suspended in 40 mL of ethanol in a round-bottom flask and held at 318 K in an ultrasonic bath (VU-09-Ya-FP-03) at a frequency of 22 kHz for 15 min. An alcohol solution of palladium chloride, as required for the preparation of a catalyst containing 5 wt % of the metal, was then slowly poured into the resulting suspension heated to a temperature of 318 K. To this end, a 25 mg weighed portion of PdCl2 was dissolved in 10 mL of ethanol acidified with 0.6 mmol of hydrochloric acid. The resulting composition was stirred with a magnetic stirrer for 45 min. The reactor was then purged with hydrogen; the attached metal was reduced with sodium tetrahydroborate (100 mg) in a hydrogen atmosphere. The reduction was conducted in a thermostatted glass reactor at a temperature of 318 K under continuous stirring with a magnetic stirrer with a Teflon anchor for 30 min. The suspension was then filtered through an ashless paper filter (FILTRAK blue ribbon) and washed with distilled water and ethanol until the mother liquor gave a negative test for chlorine ions in reaction with a 10% silver nitrate solution. Next, the catalyst was dried in a thermostatted oven at a temperature of 318 K for 2 h to obtain a black powder (Kt-2).

classification.evidence: Kt-2 catalyst prepared from sample 2 (GO + Ethylenediamine) as Pd/FGO.

material_input.summary: 10 g activated carbon suspended in 100 mL water at 313-323 K, sonicated at 318 K (22 kHz) for 15 min. PdCl2 solution (166.7 mg in 30 mL 1 M HCl) added. 160 mL 5% Li formate at 328 K added, synthesis at 328 K. After 15-20 min, heated to 343 K, settled 24 h, decanted 3-4 times, air-dried.
material_input.evidence_sentence: This catalyst was prepared as follows. Prior to the deposition of palladium, 10 g of activated carbon was suspended in 100 mL of water in a round-bottom flask at 313–323 K for 15 min and held in the ultrasonic bath (VU-09-Ya-FP-03) at 318 K and a frequency of 22 kHz for 15 min. An aqueous solution of palladium chloride required for the preparation of a catalyst containing 1 wt % of the metal was then slowly poured into the resulting suspension heated to a temperature of 318 K. A 166.7 mg weighed portion of PdCl2 was dissolved in 30 mL of 1 M hydrochloric acid. Next, 160 mL of a 5% aqueous solution of lithium formate heated to 328 K was added to the resulting mixture. Synthesis was conducted at 328 K. After 15–20 min, the reaction mixture was heated to 343 K, cooled to room temperature, and allowed to settle for 24 h. After settlement, it was subjected to decantation with distilled water 3–4 times at 24 h intervals. After the final decantation, the catalyst was air-dried at room temperature.

classification.evidence: Pd/C (M200) is described as 'palladium-containing activated carbon M200' and is used as a commercial reference catalyst; the paper makes no mention of any graphene-family component for this material.

material_input.material: 3D printed CeZrLa and CeZrLa-GO nanocatalyst structures
material_input.summary: Starting powders synthesized via CHFS (as previously described). Formulated into printing ink (54 wt% methyl cellulose polymer, 42 wt% nano-powder, 4 wt% lubrication additive), mixed using planetary centrifugal mixer, printed via direct co-extrusion, dried gradually in climate chamber and air over several days, then heat-treated in inert gas at 500°C to remove binder.
material_input.evidence_sentence: The starting powders (CeZrLa and CeZrLa/GO nanocomposites) were obtained by utilising an environmentally benign, rapid, single-step chemical synthesis route, Continuous Hydrothermal Flow Synthesis (CHFS) process, as previously described. (Kellici et al., 2018; Middelkoop et al., 2014) The CHFS as-synthesised nanomaterials were prepared into ink formulations for 3D printing from the optimal polymer/powder ratio of 54 wt% of polymer (an aqueous solution of methyl cellulose) and 42 wt% of the nano-powder that were mixed with a small amount (4 wt%) of lubrication additive using a planetary centrifugal mixer (Thinky Mixer ARE-250). Both GO-supported and unsupported nanocomposite lattices were fabricated layer by layer by the direct co-extrusion of a viscous nanocomposite paste through a syringe and a nozzle mounted on an x,y,z stage in order to construct arrays of definite geometrical patterns and morphology with excellent reproducibility. In order to obtain self-supporting stacked layers of printed fibers that maintain sufficient mechanical integrity, they were gradually dried, first in a climate chamber, then in air over the course of several days, with subsequent heat-treatment in an inert gas at 500 °C to remove the binder.

classification.evidence: Both GO-supported and unsupported nanocomposite lattices were fabricated layer by layer by direct co-extrusion; the starting powders (CeZrLa/GO nanocomposites) were obtained via CHFS. 3D printed GO-supported CeZeLa catalysts showed improved activity.

material_input.evidence_sentence: 15 mg GO was dispersed into 60 mL deionized water and ultrasonic cell crushing for 5 min to form homogeneous GO dispersion (0.25 mg/mL). Then the GO dispersion was gradually added to 60 mL PLL solution (5 mg/mL) and stirred for a while. After standing for 20 min, 45 μL N2H4 (80 %) was added into the mixture to reduce GO. To assemble PASP in the rGO/PLL, 60 mL rGO/PLL dispersion was gradually added to 60 mL PASP solution (5 mg/mL) and stirred for a while. After standing for 20 min, the resultant rGO/PLL/PASP was centrifuged at 8000 rpm and washed with pure water for 3 times. The rGO/(PLL/PASP)n nanofilms were obtained following the above self-assembly manner.

classification.evidence: rGO/(PLL/PASP)n nanofilms were made by reducing GO and alternating PLL/PASP assembly on rGO sheets via electrostatic interaction.

material_input.summary: Modified Hummer's method: 1 g graphite powder dissolved in 50 ml H2SO4, then 1 g NaNO3 added and stirred 30 min. Ice bath at ~3°C, then 6 g KMnO4 added gradually. Stirred at 35°C overnight, then 50 ml water added. After 1 h stirring, temperature raised to ~90°C. Then 280 ml warm water and 10 ml H2O2 added. Brownish-yellow color indicated GO formation. Sample washed, centrifuged, and dried at 60°C.
material_input.evidence_sentence: GO was prepared as in Fig. 1a using a modified Hummer’s method by oxidation of graphite powder, potassium permanganate, and sulfuric acid producing diamanganese heptoxide (Mn2O7) which oxidizes the double bonds of graphite forming GO [31–33]. This procedure involved dissolving 1 g of graphite powder in 50 ml of sulfuric acid H2SO4, then adding 1 g of sodium nitrate NaNO3 and stirring the mixture for 30 min. The reaction vessel was placed in an ice bath with the temperature set at approximately 3 °C. 6 g of potassium permanganate (KMnO4) was gradually added. After stirring the reaction mixture at 35 °C for a whole night, 50 ml of water was added, and after 1 h of stirring, the reaction temperature was raised to about 90 °C. After that, 280 ml of warm water and 10 ml of hydrogen peroxide H2O2 were added. The appearance of a brownish-yellow colour indicated the formation of GO. A GO sample is washed, centrifuged, and dried at 60 °C.

classification.evidence: GO was prepared as in Fig. 1a using a modified Hummer’s method by oxidation of graphite powder, potassium permanganate, and sulfuric acid producing diamanganese heptoxide (Mn2O7) which oxidizes the double bonds of graphite forming GO [31–33].

material_input.summary: Seed-mediated growth method: Seed prepared by adding 0.6 ml of 0.01 M iced NaBH4 to 7.5 ml of 0.2 M CTAB and 2.5 ml of 0.001 M HAuCl4. material_input.evidence_sentence: AuNRs were synthesized as in Fig. 1b by the seed-mediated growth method [34] in two steps. First, the seed solution is prepared by adding iced NaBH4 (0.6 ml, 0.01 M) to 7.5 ml of 0.2 M CTAB and 2.5 ml of 0.001M HAuCl4, a brownish-yellow solution is formed. After stirring, the seed was kept at room temperature. Then, using the growth solution of AuNRs prepared by CTAB (50 ml, 0.2 M), by adjusting the concentration of AgNO3 in the growth solution, two different samples with two aspect ratios of AuNRs can be obtained. So, 2 ml of AgNO3 (0.004 M for preparation of AuNRs1 and 0.002 M for preparation of AuNRs2), and HAuCl4 (50 ml of 0.001 M) were added after that in each sample and stirred at room temperature, also L-ascorbic acid (0.1 M) was then added, and that solution became colourless immediately.. Finally, add 0.8 ml of the seed solution. The growth solution was kept at room temperature. The colour of the solution gradually changed to amaranth within about 10 min. AuNRs1 and AuNRs2 started to grow for 24 h until the growth was complete. After that, the AuNRs were purified by centrifugation at 12,000 rpm for 10 min to remove excess CTAB. [35]

material_input.evidence_sentence: The devices (Fig. 1a) were prepared on a doped silicon substrate, insulated by a 500-nm-thick silicon oxide layer. Sufficiently thin graphene flakes (,0.5 nm) and suitable sizes are selected and their positions relative to the markers are recorded. Then a 0.6-nm-thick layer of Al is deposited on the substrate under ultrahigh vacuum conditions. The substrate is cooled to 77 K, to increase the uniformity of the Al layer. The layer is oxidized under 100 mbar of O2 pressure for 1 h and taken out to ambient atmosphere. Finally, using electron beam lithography the ferromagnetic contacts (50 nm thick) are fabricated.

The dark brown solution allowed to mix at 98 °C for 15 min before adding 280 ml of DI H₂O containing 7 ml of H₂O₂. material_input.summary: Graphite nanoparticles (1 g) pre-oxidized in H2SO4 (60 mL) and HNO3 (20 mL) with 100 W ultrasonication for 3 h, refluxed at 100 °C for 12 h, centrifuged at 20,000 rpm for 20 min, supernatant dialyzed (MWCO 1 kDa) for 3 days, pH adjusted to 8.
material_input.evidence_sentence: 1 g of GNPs were pre-oxidized in a concentrated H2SO4 (60 mL) and HNO3 (20 mL) solution for 3 h under mild ultrasonication (100 W). Then, the solution went through refluxing for 12 h at 100 °C. After cooling to room temperature (RT), the mixture was diluted with deionized water. The suspension was centrifuged at 20,000 rpm for 20 min, and a supernatant was carefully obtained. The supernatant was dialyzed to remove acidic ions and then adjusted to pH 8 by adding a certain amount of a diluted NaOH solution. The solution was further dialyzed to completely remove the salt ions in a dialysis bag (molecular weight cut-off: 1 kDa) for 3 days.

classification.evidence: graphene oxide quantum dots (GOQDs)

material_input.summary: 10 mL of 0.01 mg/mL GOQDs reacted with 2.5 mL each of 20 mM EDC and 5 mM NHS for 2 h, then 20 nM aptamer added and stirred overnight at RT, purified by dialysis (MWCO 50,000 Da).
material_input.evidence_sentence: To functionalize the GOQD by a Cu2+ specific aptamer, 2.5 mL of 20 mM EDC and 2.5 mL of 5 mM NHS were added to 10 mL of a 0.01 mg/mL GOQD solution. The mixture was stirred with a magnetic bar for 2 h. Then, a 20 nM aptamer solution was added, and was kept stirring in a dark chamber at RT overnight. Afterwards, the dialysis bag with molecular weight cut-off of 50,000 Da was used to purify the unconjugated aptamer (the molecular weight of the aptamer: 13189.7 Da) to produce the GOQD-aptamer solution.

classification.evidence: Amino-modified Cu2+-specific aptamers were linked on the GOQDs through an amide bond to produce GOQD-aptamer conjugates; the UCPL of GOQD was utilized as an optical biosensor for detecting aptamer-based metal ions.

material_input.summary: Graphite powder (1 g) reacted with 25 mL 98% H2SO4 at RT for 12 h, then 500 mg NaNO3 at 0°C for 30 min, then 3.65 g KMnO4 added slowly (temp <20°C) and stirred 2 h, held at 35±3°C for 30 min, then 46 mL water added over 25 min while heating to 98°C for 15 min, finally 27.5 mL water + 3.5 mL 30% H2O2 added, washed, filtered, exfoliated by bath sonication for 2 h, dispersed at 1 mg/mL.
material_input.evidence_sentence: GO was synthesized from graphite powder by a modified Hummers method. In a typical synthesis, graphite powder (1 g) and H2SO4 (25 mL, 98 wt.%) were mixed in a 250 mL round-bottom flask and stirred at room temperature for 12 h. Afterwards, of NaNO3 (500 mg) was added into the mixture and stirred at 0 °C for 30 min. Subsequently, KMnO4 (3.65 g) was slowly added to prevent the temperature to exceed 20 °C and the suspension was stirred for 2 h. After that, the temperature of the system was kept at 35 ± 3 °C for 30 min, then, 46 mL of water was slowly added into the suspension within 25 min, and the temperature of the system was increased to 98 °C and maintained for 15 min. At last, 27.5 mL of water and H2O2 (3.5 mL, 30%) were added, followed by water washing and filtration. The as-prepared graphite oxide was exfoliated in water by bath ultrasonication for 2 h, to form GO. Finally, the as purified GO was dispersed well in water (1 mg/mL).

classification.evidence: GO was synthesized from graphite powder by a modified Hummers method. The as-prepared graphite oxide was exfoliated in water by bath ultrasonication for 2 h, to form GO. Finally, the as purified GO was dispersed well in water (1 mg/mL).

material_input.summary: 15 mg GO dispersed in 15 mL water by sonication for 1 h; Ag(NH3)2OH prepared by adding 0.55 M NH3 to AgNO3 (5-15 mL, 0.06 M); mixed with GO/glucose, stirred 0.5 h, rested at RT for 2 h, centrifuged, washed, dried at 60°C overnight. Ag loadings: 8.5-34.8 wt.%.
material_input.evidence_sentence: Firstly, GO powder (15.0 mg) was dispersed in water (15.0 mL) by sonication for 1 h to form a stable GO colloid and then glucose was added to this solution under stirring. Second, ammonia (0.55 mol.L−1) was added slowly to a solution of AgNO3 (5.0, 8.0, 10.0, 12.0, and 15.0 mL, respectively, 0.06 mol.L−1) until the AgOH/Ag2O precipitate dissolved to form Ag(NH3)2OH solution. Subsequently, the Ag(NH3)2OH solution was mixed with the GO and glucose-containing solution. After being stirred for 0.5 h, the mixture was kept undisturbed at room temperature for 2 h. The slurry-like product was centrifuged and washed with excess water repeatedly to remove any impurities. Finally, the obtained product was dried overnight in an oven at 60 °C, and then GO-Ag nanocomposites with different Ag loading were obtained. By thermogravimetric analysis (TG), content of Ag in the GO-Ag nanocomposites were 8.5, 13.7, 21.6, 29.6 and 34.8 wt.%, respectively.

classification.evidence: GO-Ag nanocomposite was prepared by reducing silver ions directly on GO with glucose; Ag nanoparticles were completely distributed on GO sheets, indicating GO acts as a support for Ag nanoparticles.

material_input.summary: GCE polished with 1.0, 0.3, 0.05 μm alumina, cleaned ultrasonically for 30 min; 5 mg GO-Ag dispersed in 10 mL DMF by sonication for 30 min (0.5 mg/mL); 5 μL coated on GCE, dried under IR; electropolymerization in PBS (pH 7.5) with 1.0×10^-3 mol/L lysine by CV from -1.0 to 0.4 V for 30 cycles at 100 mV/s.
material_input.evidence_sentence: A glassy carbon electrode (GCE) was first polished stepwise with 1.0, 0.3 and 0.05 μm alumina powders, and then washed with ethanol-water (1:1, V/V) and double distilled water in an ultrasonic bath for 30 min. Different Ag loading of GO-Ag hybrids materials (5.0 mg) were dispersed in DMF (10 mL) with the aid of ultrasonic bath for 30 min to give a 0.5 mg.mL−1 black suspension. Then the prepared suspension (5.0 μL) was coated onto the fresh GCE surface using a micropipette, followed by evaporating the solvent under an infrared lamp. The obtained electrode was denoted as GO-Ag/GCE. The electropolymerization of l-lysine on GO-Ag/GCE was performed by dipping the above GO-Ag/GCE into PBS buffer solution (pH 7.5) containing 1.0 × 10−3 mol/L l-lysine by cyclic potential scanning from −1.0 to 0.4 V for 30 cycles with a scan rate of 100 mV s−1. The final obtained electrode was denoted as GO-Ag/PLL/GCE.

classification.evidence: GO-Ag hybrid matrix was coated on the GCE and covered with poly-l-lysine to form the dopamine-sensing GO-Ag/PLL/GCE electrode.

material_input.summary: Room temperature acid precipitation of Na2WO4·2H2O with PdCl2 at 0.5, 1.0, and 1.5 mol% Pd doping. GO synthesized via modified Hummer's method and attached to Pd1.5 via electrostatic interactions using PDDA with GO concentrations of 0.5, 1.0, and 2.0 wt%.
material_input.evidence_sentence: Room temperature acid precipitation was used to prepare pure and Pd doped WO3 nanostructures where Na2WO4·2H2O and PdCl2 were the precursors of WO3 and Pd, respectively. Different concentrations of Pd (0.5, 1.0 and 1.5 mol%) were used and labeled as Pd0.5, Pd1.0, and Pd1.5, respectively. Graphite oxide (GO) was synthesized by means of modified Hummer's method [15]. The attachment of Pd1.5 on to RGO sheets was attained by electrostatic interactions by using PDDA. Samples with different concentration of GO (0.5, 1.0 and 2.0 wt%) were labelled as PdG0.5, PdG1.0 and PdG2.0, respectively.

material_input.evidence_sentence: The synthesis process of Fe₃O₄@SiO₂/GO/β-CD was depicted in Fig. 1 A. β-CD was grafted on Fe₃O₄@SiO₂/GO through hydrogen bond between the hydroxyl of β-CD and oxygen-containing functional groups of GO in water.

material_input.summary: Graphite (2 g, 99.99%) was dispersed in 50 ml concentrated H₂SO₄ containing 7 g KMnO₄. material_input.summary: GO dispersed in water (2 mg/ml) and sonicated 2 h. GA-PEG synthesized: GA (47 mg, 0.1 mmol) + NH2-PEG-NH2 (200 mg, 0.1 mmol) with EDC (58 mg, 0.3 mmol) and NHS (35 mg, 0.3 mmol) in DCM at room temperature. GA-PEG (25 mg) conjugated to NGO (0.5 mg/ml, 20 ml) via EDC (100 mg) with sonication (10 min). PAMAM dendrimer (generation 4.0, 50 mg) added, stirred 24 h, dialyzed (300 kDa) 3 days.
material_input.evidence_sentence: 50 mg GO was dispersed in 25 ml redistilled water and sonicated for 2 h to prepare monolayer of NGO homogeneous dispersion (2 mg/ml). GA-PEG was synthesized by the amidation of GA (J&K) and NH2-PEG-NH2 in the presence of EDC (J&K) and NHS (J&K). Under argon protection, GA (47 mg, 0.1 mmol) was dissolved in anhydrous dichloromethane, followed by addition of 1-ethyl-3-(3-(dimethylamino) propyl) carbodiimide hydro-chloride (EDC, 58 mg, 0.3 mmol) and N-hydroxy succinylamide (NHS, 35 mg, 0.3 mmol) into the flake. In order to avoid the generation of GA-PEG-GA, the reaction mixture was then dropwise added into an anhydrous dichloromethane solution of NH2-PEG-NH2 (200 mg, 0.1 mmol) under argon protection at room temperature. Afterwards, EDC (100 mg) was mixed with NGO dispersion (0.5 mg/ml, 20 ml) and sonicated for 10 min, GA-PEG (25 mg) was added. After sonication for another 10 min, PAMAM Dendrimer (generation 4.0 solution, sigma, 50 mg) was added into the flake by stirring. The mixture was stirred for 24 h, and then subjected to dialysis (300 kDa) for 3 days.

material_input.summary: Modified Hummer's method: 2 g graphite flakes and 1 g NaNO3 in 140 mL concentrated H2SO4, ice bath 30 min; 6 g KMnO4 added slowly, ice-cold 4 h; 35 °C water bath 3 h; 200 mL water added, stirred at 90 °C for 30 min; H2O2 added until no gas; filtered, washed with 1 M HCl and water until neutral pH; dried overnight at 45 °C.
material_input.evidence_sentence: Graphene oxide was synthesized via a modified Hummer's method by oxidizing purified graphite ( Feicht et al., 2019 ). The graphite flakes (2 g) and sodium nitrate (1 g) were put into a solution of concentrated H 2 SO 4 (140 mL) in an ice bath and stirred for 30 min. Subsequently, potassium permanganate (6 g) was slowly added under ice-cold conditions and mixed for 4 h. The mixture was stirred for 3 h in a 35 °C water bath to form a brown paste, and 200 mL of deionized water was slowly added and stirred at 90 °C for Half-hour. Finally, hydrogen peroxide was added to the mixture until no gas was generated from the reaction, after that the color of the mixture was changed from brown to brilliant yellow. The mixture was filtered and washed by HCl (1 M) and deionized water several times until pH became neutral. The graphene oxide product was dried overnight at 45 °C.

classification.evidence: Graphene oxide was synthesized via a modified Hummer's method by oxidizing purified graphite (Feicht et al., 2019).

material_input.evidence_sentence: Concentrated H2 SO4 was added to a mixture of graphite nanofibers (1.0 g, 1 equiv wt) and NaNO3 (0.75 g, 0.5 equiv wt). The mixture was cooled to 0 °C. KMnO4 , which acts as a strong oxidising agent (3.0 g, 3 equiv wt), was slowly added in portions to maintain the reaction temperature below 20 °C. The reaction was increased to 35 °C and stirred for 30 min, after which Milli-Q water (175 mL) was slowly added. This process produced a large exothermic reaction, which raised the temperature to 98 °C. External heating was introduced to maintain the reaction temperature at 98 °C for an additional 15 min. The mixture was then cooled in a water bath to ambient temperatures (∼21 °C) for 10 min. An additional 1.5 mL of Milli-Q water and 1.5 ml of 30% H2 O2 were added... filtered by centrifugation (2500 rpm, 1 h) and washed repeatedly with both 1 M HCl and Milli-Q water. Finally, the product was dried at 60 °C for 24 h to obtain GO.

material_input.summary: Three-step synthesis: 1) GO by modified Hummers method - 2.0 g graphite in 50 mL concentrated H2SO4, 6.0 g KMnO4 added over ~2.5 h below 15 °C, stirred 24 h at 35 °C, 7 mL 30% H2O2 added, centrifuged at 10,000 rpm for 20 min, washed with 0.2 M HCl/water/ethanol, dried at 50 °C; 2) mGO by solvothermal method - 0.75 g GO ultrasonicated >2 h in 100 mL ethylene glycol, 3.0 g NaAc and 1.3 g FeCl3·6H2O added at 30 °C, stirred 40 min, heated at 190 °C for 6 h, filtered, washed, dried at 60 °C for 48 h; 3) EDTA functionalization - 1.4 g mGO in 25 mL 12% HAC, 4.0 g EDTA in 130 mL ethylene glycol added, stirred 4 h at 60 °C, washed, magnetic separation, dried at 60 °C for 24 h.
material_input.evidence_sentence: The synthesis of EDTA-mGO consists of three steps, which are schematically presented in Scheme 1. First, GO was fabricated by oxidizing natural graphite according to the modified Hummers method [19]. In brief, 2.0 g graphite powder was added in a concentrated H2SO4 solution (50 mL) under potent stirring and ice-water bath conditions. While maintaining vigorous stirring, 6.0 g KMnO4 was gradually added over approximately 2.5 h and the temperature was carefully controlled to keep the reaction temperature below 15 °C. The suspension was persistently churned for 24 h at 35 °C. Subsequently, 7 mL of 30% H2O2 solution was dropwise added into the mixture until the color of the suspension changed into yellow. After centrifugation at 10,000 rpm for 20 min, the mixture was washed with 0.2 M HCl followed by Milli-Q water and ethyl alcohol for several times. The final product was dried in a vacuum oven at 50 °C, thus GO was obtained. The mGO composite was prepared by solvothermal method [20]. Typically, the as-prepared GO (0.75 g) was dispersed by ultrasonication in 100 ml of ethylene glycol for more than 2 h. Then 3.0 g NaAc and 1.3 g FeCl3·6H2O were added in the above mixture at 30 °C. After stirring for about 40 min, the solution was kept at 190 °C for 6 h. The black precipitate was filtered and washed with distilled water and ethanol, and dried in vacuum oven at 60 °C for 48 h, mGO was thus obtained. Then, 1.4 g mGO nanoparticles were dispersed into 25 mL of HAC (12%) aqueous solution in a flask. After mixing well, 4.0 g EDTA was added to disperse in 130 mL of Ethylene glycol [16]. After stirring for 4 h at 60 °C, the mixture was washed several times by ethanol and water alternatively and collected by magnetic separation. Finally, the nanoparticles were dried in vacuum oven at 60 °C for 24 h.

classification.evidence: EDTA-mGO was fabricated by binding chelating groups (EDTA) onto Fe3O4/graphene oxide; magnetic Fe3O4 nanoparticles are well dispersed on GO nanosheets (few layers thick)

material_input.evidence_sentence: The GO/CDots/BiOI were synthesized by a mixed precipitation method according to previous reports [12,24] . In a typical procedure, 4 mmol Bi(NO3)3·5H2O was dissolved into a solution contained 36 mL deionized water and 4 mL acetic acid to obtain solution A. 4 mmol IL [C16mim]I, 2 mL CDots (4 mg/L) and 4 mL GO (5 mg/L) aqueous were all dispersed into 40 mL alcohol to obtain solution B. The solution B was added into solution A dropwise under stirring condition and then the mixing solution was stirred for another 1 h. Subsequently, the resultant product was centrifuged and washed adequately with alcohol and deionized water three times. Finally, the resulting precipitates were dried in a vacuum at 60 °C overnight and ground into fine powder before further characterization and using in photocatalytic reactions.

classification.evidence: Non-metallic graphene oxide (GO) co-doped BiOI ternary system (GO/CDots/BiOI) synthesized via mixed precipitation; GO acts as electron reservoir and transporter to inhibit photogenerated charge recombination.

material_input.summary: Sol-gel synthesis using CdCl2·2.5H2O (6 × 10-4 M) and Na2SeSO3 (0.02 M) with Pluronic F-127, acetic acid, HCl, and ethanol; heated at 40 °C for 12 h then 65 °C for 24 h; calcined at 320 °C for 4 h (1 °C/min ramp).
material_input.evidence_sentence: In precise, the Sol-gel process started by combining a 2.5 g of F127 to a magnetically stirred mixture of 7.0 mL of CH3COOH plus 0.80 mL of HCl in a 50 mL of CH3CH2OH. Formerly, 0.1368 g of CdCl2·2.5H2O (6 × 10-4 M) dissolved in this solution under moderate stirring for 30 min. Consequently, a freshly prepared solution of 20 mL Na2SeSO3 (0.02 M) was poured drop-by-drop and kept under stir for another 60 min. Next, the whole sol relocated into a Petri dish. The dish was heated in the ambiance for 12 h at 40 °C, and subsequently, the temperature raised to 65 °C for another 24 h. Finally, the as-produced materials were treated at 320 °C for 4 h in ambiance with a rate of 1 °C min-1 for heating and cooling processes to eliminate the F127 and to obtain highly crystalline CdSe.

classification.evidence: The requested target CdSe nanoparticles is synthesized via sol-gel process using CdCl2·2.5H2O and Na2SeSO3 with no graphene component. material_input.summary: Improved Hummer's method: pre-oxidation of graphite with K2S2O8/P2O5 in H2SO4 for 6 h; further oxidation with NaNO3/KMnO4 in H2SO4 at 35-40 °C; yields ~1.5 g GO.
material_input.evidence_sentence: GO was prepared similarly with the improved Hummer's method. Briefly, 2.0 g of graphite was firstly pre-oxidized by an assortment of 4.0 g of K2S2O8 and 4.0 g of P2O5 in 10 mL of H2SO4 and stirred for 6 h to obtain pre-oxidized graphite. Then, the pre-oxidized graphite filtered after washing with 250 mL of distilled water. The primary GO furtherly oxidized in another mixture of 120 mL of H2SO4, 1.0 g of NaNO3, and 12.0 g of KMnO4 surrounded by ice. Then, the temperature of this combination increased to 35-40 °C after slowly dropping 140 mL of distilled water for 30 min until reaching about 100 °C by external heating. An excess of water (about 250 mL) added continuously to cool down the temperature. The produced GO was gained by decantation, washing, and centrifugation to obtain about 1.5 g of yield.

classification.evidence: GO was prepared similarly with the improved Hummer's method. The produced GO was gained by decantation, washing, and centrifugation to obtain about 1.5 g of yield.

material_input.summary: GO (5-20 wt% relative to CdSe) ultrasonicated in ethanol for 30 min; 100 mg CdSe dispersed in ethanol; CdSe added dropwise at 1 mL/min over 50 min; heated at 60 °C for 10 h to reduce GO and attach CdSe.
material_input.evidence_sentence: First, the pre-calculated amount of GO ultrasonically spread in a beaker containing 20 mL of CH3CH2OH for 30 min. At the same time, 100 mg of CdSe was also ultrasonically dispersed in another 30 mL of CH3CH2OH. Then, the CdSe dispersion was slowly dropped (1 mL min-1) by the burette until the whole process completed within 50 min. The mixture was then heated to 60 °C for 10 h in a thermostat drier to reduce the GO and thereby to leave the CdSe attached to the rGO. The rGO contents were varied to obtain different CdSe/rGO nanocomposite samples with rGO weight fractions of 5, 10, 15, and 20 wt%.

classification.evidence: The mixture was then heated to 60 °C for 10 h in a thermostat drier to reduce the GO and thereby to leave the CdSe attached to the rGO.

material_input.evidence_sentence: GO was synthesized via modified Hummers method [26,27]. Graphite flakes is used as raw material with chemical reagents including NaNO3, H2SO4, KMnO4, H2O2 and HCl.
